task_completion.description: Generate descriptive statistics and distribution plots for the 5 specified variables.
task_completion.visual_evidence: JASP results panel showing a 'Descriptive Statistics' table with Mean, Std. Deviation, Minimum, and Maximum for Happiness Score, GDP per Capita, Family, Life Expectancy, and Freedom, along with their corresponding Distribution Plots.

task_completion.description: Generate a correlation matrix with significance flags and a heatmap.
task_completion.visual_evidence: JASP results panel showing a 'Pearson Correlations' table for the 5 variables with asterisks indicating significance, and a Correlation Heatmap plot.

task_completion.description: Run Model 1 multiple regression with collinearity diagnostics, residual plots, and partial correlations.
task_completion.visual_evidence: JASP results panel showing 'Linear Regression' with Happiness Score as the dependent variable and the 4 predictors. The output must include 'Collinearity Diagnostics' (Tolerance/VIF), 'Part and Partial Correlations', a 'Q-Q Plot Standardized Residuals', and a 'Residuals vs. Predicted' plot.

task_completion.description: Create a computed column for GDP_squared.
task_completion.visual_evidence: JASP data spreadsheet view showing a new column named 'GDP_squared' (or similar) with a formula indicating GDP per Capita multiplied by itself, or the resulting squared values.

task_completion.description: Run Model 2 regression including the new GDP_squared variable.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A second 'Linear Regression' results section in JASP showing 'GDP_squared' added as a predictor alongside GDP per Capita, Family, Life Expectancy, and Freedom.

task_completion.description: Save the JASP analysis file to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: JASP 'Save As' dialog or a terminal/file manager window showing 'happiness_regression_pipeline.jasp' saved in the '/home/ga/Documents/JASP/' directory.

task_completion.description: Write the final text report with the required statistical values and conclusion.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor open with '/home/ga/Documents/JASP/regression_report.txt' containing the Pearson r (~0.81), Model 1 R-squared (~0.806), Model 2 R-squared (~0.812), highest VIF (~26.1), GDP_squared p-value (~0.031), and a conclusion stating Model 2 fits better.

integrity.description: The agent used the JASP GUI to perform the analysis.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the JASP application menus, dialogs, and results panel, rather than using a Python script or terminal commands to bypass the GUI.

integrity.description: The statistical results in the JASP output match the expected dataset.
integrity.visual_evidence: The JASP results panel shows values consistent with the World Happiness dataset (e.g., N=155, R-squared values around 0.80-0.81) and not fabricated or mocked data.

integrity.description: The values in the text report are derived from the JASP analysis.
integrity.visual_evidence: The numbers typed into the text report match the numbers visible in the JASP results panel screenshots.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Ground truth values for the World Happiness dataset (N=155): Pearson r between GDP per Capita and Happiness Score is approximately 0.8125. Model 1 R-squared is approximately 0.806. Model 2 R-squared is approximately 0.812. The highest VIF in Model 1 is for GDP per Capita, approximately 26.1 (JASP may show 25-27 depending on internal precision). The p-value for GDP_squared in Model 2 is approximately 0.031 (significant at alpha=0.05). Model 2 is the better fitting model due to the higher R-squared and significant quadratic term. The computed column GDP_squared must be created using JASP's computed column feature (+ button in column headers).
